channel.title: Captain Dadders — Flight Expeditions
channel.description: Following the Lewis & Clark Corps of Discovery route by air — MSFS 2024 expedition blog.

item.title: Leg 6: Clark's Country — 4KT4 to KJVY
item.description: A peaceful cruise down the Ohio past the Milton-Madison Bridge and the massive Clifty Creek power plant, landing at Clark Regional Airport in Clark County, Indiana — the county, and the airport, that bear the name of William Clark's older brother George Rogers Clark, the Revolutionary War hero who secured the Northwest Territory and founded Clarksville just downstream.

item.title: Leg 5: Queen City to the Fossil Lick — KLUK to 4KT4
item.description: Flying past downtown Cincinnati — the Great American Ballpark, the Bengals' stadium, and the skyline — then following the Ohio River southwest into Kentucky, landing at Steele's Bottom near Warsaw, close to Big Bone Lick, where Lewis collected mastodon fossils for President Jefferson before the expedition departed.

item.title: Leg 4: River Highway to Cincinnati — I41 to KLUK
item.description: Past West Virginia at last, with Kentucky on the left and Ohio on the right. The Ohio River in 1803 was a bustling highway of westward migration — settlers, merchants, traders, and Native peoples all sharing the corridor. River barges still ply the same waters today. Arriving at Lunken Field on the east side of Cincinnati.

item.title: Leg 3: Above the Morning Fog — 75D to I41
item.description: A memorable flight through morning fog along the Ohio River from New Martinsville, West Virginia to Tri-State Airport at Huntington. The river valley was draped in mist with hilltops emerging above the clouds — exactly the kind of scenery Lewis would have seen pushing downriver in the autumn of 1803.

item.title: Leg 2: Wheeling and the Mound — 1G8 to 75D
item.description: Following the Ohio River south from Toronto, Ohio through the industrial river towns of Steubenville and Wheeling, West Virginia — passing the remarkable Grave Creek Mound, the largest surviving Adena burial mound in the United States, before landing at New Martinsville.

item.title: Leg 1: Departing Pittsburgh — 31D to 1G8
item.description: The expedition gets underway. Following the Ohio River from Inter County Airport past The Point in Pittsburgh to Eddie Dew Memorial Airpark in Toronto, Ohio — the same ground Lewis and Clark covered August 31 to September 5, 1803.
